name: Little Phyllo Cheesecakes
recipeInstructions:
Place one sheet of phyllo on a damp towel (keep remaining phyllo covered). Lightly brush the phyllo with melted butter. Layer three more sheets of phyllo on the first, brushing each sheet with butter. Repeat to make another stack of four sheets of phyllo. Cut each stack of phyllo into 3-inch squares using kitchen shears. Brush miniature muffin cups with melted butter. Place one square of layered phyllo into each muffin cup, pressing gently in the center to form a pastry shell. Bake at 350 degrees for 8 to 10 minutes or until golden.
Gently remove from the pan and let cool on wire racks. Combine cream cheese, powdered sugar, orange rind, and 1 tablespoon of orange juice in a small mixing bowl; beat with an electric mixer until blended and smooth. Spoon 1-1/2 tablespoons of cream cheese mixture into each pastry shell. Combine orange marmalade and 2 tablespoons of orange juice; top each cheesecake with 1/2 teaspoon of the orange marmalade mixture. Yield: 40 pastries.
NOTE: Phyllo shells may be made up to 2 days in advance and stored in an airtight container. Fill shells up to 4 hours before serving, and keep chilled until ready to serve.
